title: core.WorktreePhase() omits test-amplification, so the phase cannot author .go files where phase_bindings.go's own comment says it must
summary: go/internal/core/worktree.go:196-198 defines WorktreePhase(p Phase) bool { return p == PhaseTDD || p == PhaseBuild } — used by the role guard (go/internal/guards/role.go:83) as the ONLY way a phase's Write/Edit into the active worktree is allowed outside cs.WorkspacePath. test-amplification is not PhaseTDD or PhaseBuild, so every Write/Edit the phase makes into the worktree's go/ tree is DENIED with 'role guard: phase=test-amplification may not write outside workspace .../runs/cycle-<N> ... pass --bypass to override in an emergency'. But phase_bindings.go:329-345's own comment says the opposite is true: '// tdd, build, AND test-amplification all author .go that the audit gofmt gate scans' (normalizeBuildWorktree gofmt-normalizes the worktree after every worktree phase specifically because test-amplification is assumed to be one). This is a live-drift / replicated-belief defect: the phase_bindings.go comment and the WorktreePhase predicate disagree about test-amplification's write scope, and nothing catches the mismatch until a test-amplification agent actually tries to add a new test file.
root_cause: test-amplification is a config-only, dynamically-inserted Evaluate-archetype phase (per feedback_phases_are_config_only — phase-building is config-only, zero Go constants), so it has no dedicated core.Phase constant to add to WorktreePhase's switch/OR-chain the way PhaseTDD/PhaseBuild do. The exact same situation already exists one function below it: LeakRecoverablePhase (worktree.go:212-221) handles 'bug-reproduction' (also config-only, no dedicated constant) via the literal Phase("bug-reproduction") alongside the named constants. WorktreePhase was never given the equivalent Phase("test-amplification") arm, so the write-permission axis silently diverged from the leak-recovery axis for this one phase.
fix: ROOT (single-source, mirrors existing precedent in the same file): in go/internal/core/worktree.go, change WorktreePhase to `return p == PhaseTDD || p == PhaseBuild || p == Phase("test-amplification")`, following the exact Phase("bug-reproduction") literal pattern LeakRecoverablePhase already uses two functions below for the same class of config-only phase. This is a control-plane file change (guards.IsProtectedSurface likely covers go/internal/core/ — verify) so it may itself require human-gated `evolve ship --class manual` outside a cycle, same boundary Task 2 (guards-role-hermetic-home, this cycle's build-report) hit for role.go.
impact_observed: Cycle 617's test-amplification phase hit this directly: Write of a new _amplify_test.go file into go/internal/adapters/storage/ was DENIED by the role guard. Cycle 605's test-amplification-report.md documents the same denial and worked around it by authoring files under the phase workspace and `cp`-ing them into the real package directory via Bash (unhooked — the PreToolUse guard hook only wraps the Write/Edit tools, not arbitrary Bash file ops). Cycle 608's test-amplification phase verdict was SKIPPED — plausibly the same root cause without the workaround being found that time. Every future test-amplification phase either has to rediscover the cp workaround or silently produces zero amplification value.
connects_to: fable5_deep_scan (live-drift proofs: cyclehealth kind-filter, retro backfill filename — same class of comment/code divergence)
per_phase_integrity (ADR-0065, self-heal precedent for control-plane files)
files: go/internal/core/phase_bindings.go:329-345 (normalizeBuildWorktree's comment asserting test-amplification already has worktree write scope)
